column1: It had been Fausto's dream to live in the UK since he had visited as a child and fallen in love with it. It was so different from his_____________country of Portugal, and he liked British culture and the way people spoke English.
When the opportunity to study there came up, he_____________at the chance.

The day had finally come to meet his host family. Having just arrived at Heathrow airport, Fausto was excited to meet them, although he was also a bit apprehensive. But he was on an adventure, and that was to be enjoyed_________all.

He knew a few things about the family. The father, Donald Hines, worked as computer_____________, which Fausto thought was really cool, and the mother, Diane, worked as a nurse in the emergency care centre of a hospital. The couple hd one son, Martin, who was the same age as Fausto.
Although he had been studying English for years, Fausto wasn't very good at speaking the language. He had trouble____________certain words and spoke rather slowly.
His reading and writing skills were excellent though, and he understood spoken English perfectly. He hoped his lack of speaking skills wouldn't interfere______________his communication for long.
As he excited the flight terminal, he saw Diane waiting for him with the rest of the family. She greeted him with open arms and give him a big hug, which helped to____________Fausto's nerves a lot!
